construct_verdict_reason: Most items measure attitudes toward conjugal unions, but p33 is a behavioral disclosure item that measures past conduct rather than attitudes or normative orientations.

items.reason: Directly measures criteria and motivations for selecting a conjugal partner, core to attitudes about forming unions.
Measures family influence on partner selection, relevant to normative orientations toward conjugal union formation.
Measures personal motivations for initiating a conjugal union, directly aligned with attitudes about forming partnerships.
Measures perceived causes of extramarital relations, relevant to attitudes about conjugal union stability and maintenance.
Measures actual past behavior (whether respondent has had affairs) rather than attitudes, normative orientations, or tolerance toward infidelity.
Measures tolerance and conditions for accepting infidelity, directly relevant to attitudes about conjugal union stability and normative orientations.
Measures perceived causes of divorce, directly aligned with attitudes about conjugal union dissolution and stability.
